steps.title: Find the common factor
steps.content:
Each term has a factor of $x^3$ because the smallest exponent of $x$ among the three terms is $3$.
$$6x^5-4x^4+11x^3$$

steps.title: Factor it out
steps.content: Divide each term by $x^3$ to get the expression inside the parentheses. $$6x^5-4x^4+11x^3=x^3(6x^2-4x+11)$$ The answer is B.
